##### 9.13.2.16.2 AON Power States Programming Guidelines
- SPE- IDLE Entry Sequence
- Agent
- Step
- Cortex-R5 SW
- Set up AO-PM with power state transition command by writing to AOPM_TGT_PWR _STATE
register.
- STATE == IDLE
- TCM_ACTIVE (implicit for IDLE mode)
Set up AO-CAR with power state transition info by writing to registers described in the Clocks Chapter to set up the clock source to switch to and the power state of the AOPLL. It could be either of these registers:
- TGT_IDLE_SHALLOW
- TGT_IDLE_DEEP
- Cortex-R5 SW
Execute WFI.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Agent
- Step
- Clock gating Logic
All second level clock gating controls kick in at this stage except the TCM clocks, backbone clocks for DMA, NOC branches that may be actively used by the DMA during this time along with any active I/O clock(s). Software may additionally FLCG I/O clock sources or switch these to OSC or 32K. Software accordingly programs TGT_IDLE before executing WFI so that AO-PM or AO-CAR hardware does not turn the PLL off when any of the I/O controllers are actively using these clock sources.
- AO-DMA
DMA goes idle and gets clock gated at the second level.
- AXI-S
AXI-S port goes idle as DMA and I/O controllers go idle.
- Cortex-R5
- AO-PM
Engage the second level clock gates on TCM clocks.
- AO-PM
Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to a slower clock.
- AO-CAR
- Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to TGT_IDLE_SHALLOW sources if enabled. Place AO-PLL in
TGT_IDLE_SHALLOW mode. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
- Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register. Set CURRENT_STATE == IDLE_SHALLOW and other fields as
per entry sequence.
- AO-PM
Check for AOPM_POWER_CFG_MASK_TGT_DEEP_IDLE. If not DISABLE-d, enter DEEP_IDLE state.
- AO-PM
Switch Cortex-R5 clocks to TGT_IDLE_DEEP. Place AO-PLL in low power TGT_IDLE_DEEP mode.
- AO-CAR
Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to TGT_IDLE_SOURCE if enabled. Place AO-PLL in TGT_IDLE mode. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register. Set CURRENT_STATE == IDLE_DEEP. Other fields as per entry sequence.
- SPE-IDLE Exit Sequence
Once an exit condition is triggered:
- Agent
- Step
- Cortex- R5
- Detects interrupt from AVIC or detects request on AXI-S port to come out of Standby mode
- AO-PM
Release TCM clock gates within three cycles of nCLKSTOPPED getting deasserted. Detect exit from the SPE-Idle state and capture the cause for wake up in a status register. If the wake up is due to an SC7 wake event: 1. 2. Re-enable the DEEP_IDLE override in hardware. Optionally raise an interrupt to SPE.
- AO-PM
Take AOPLL out of low-power mode. Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks back to the mode in which the clocks were running prior to entering this SPE-Idle state.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Agent
- Step
- AO-CAR
Place AOPLL in TGT_ACTIVE or TGT_ACTIVE_IRQFIQ mode. Switch Cortex-R5 clocks to TGT_ACTIVE or TGT_ACTIVE_IRFIQ source. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register. Set CURRENT_STATE == ACTIVE or ACTIVE_IRQFIQ.
- SPE-STANDBY Entry Sequence
- Agent
- Step
- Cortex- R5 SW
Set up AO-PM with power state transition command by writing to: AOPM_TGT_PWR _STATE register.
- STATE == STANDBY
RAM_RET or RAIL_RET enabled.
- RAM_RET = RAM ret mode
- RAIL_RET = AON rail retention
Set RAM_RET and/or RAIL_RET. Set up AO-CAR with power state transition info for clock sources and AOPLL.
- TGT_STBY_SHALLOW
- TGT_STBY_DEEP
- Cortex- R5 SW
Execute WFI.
- Cortex- R5 HW
- AXI-S port goes idle
- AO-PM
Execute the entry steps for entering SPE-Idle. During these steps, AO-PM requests AO-CAR to use TGT_STBY_SHALLOW commands. SPE-Standby entry is stepped through SPE-Idle. Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register.
- CURRENT_STATE = STBY_SHALLOW_IDLE
Optionally, use the DMA_ACTIVE (MC-side) indicator from the SPE-DMA to infer idleness.
- AO-PM
Block TCM accesses from AXI-S port upon expiry of the retention entry delay timer.
- AO-PM
Block interrupts from reaching the Cortex-R5 from VIC.
- AO-PM
Check if RAM_RET is enabled. Handshakes with PMC.
- PMC
Completes RAM retention sequencing and ACKs back to PM.
- AO-PM
Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register with STATE == STBY_SHALLOW.
- AO-PM
Check for AOPM_POWER_CFG_MASK_TGT_DEEP_STANDBY. If DISABLE-d, enter DEEP_STANDBY state.
- AO-PM
Switch Cortex-R5 clocks to TGT_STBY source. Place AO-PLL in low power mode TGT_STBY.
- AO-CAR
Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to TGT_STBY if enabled. Switch non Cortex-R5 clocks to OSC if they are sourced from AO-PLL. Place AO-PLL in TGT_STBY mode. Acknowledge AO-PM.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Agent
- Step
- AO-PM
Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register. Set CURRENT_STATE == STANDBY_DEEP. Other fields as per entry sequence.
- SPE-STANDBY Exit Sequence
- Agent
- Step
- Cortex- R5
Detects interrupt from AVIC or detects request on AXI-S port to come out of Standby mode.
- AO-PM
Release TCM clock gates within three cycles of nCLKSTOPPED getting deasserted. Detect exit from SPE-Standby state and capture the cause for wake-up in a status register.
- If the exit condition is due to an SC7 wake request from PMC
1. 2. Re-enable DEEP_STANDBY override in hardware. Optionally raise an interrupt to SPE. Before taking the rail out of retention, aon_cpu_clk, aon_nic_clk and aon_apb_clk clocks should be switched back to at least the OSC clock if they were running off 32 kHz. This is to not incur extra latency on the exit path by operating the logic that controls rail voltage at a very slow clock.
- AO-PM
- Switch aon_cpu_clk, aon_nic_clk and aon_apb_clk back to OSC and wait for acknowledgement
from AO-CAR.
- AO-CAR
Switch AOPLL and Cortex-R5 clocks to TGT_STBY_SHALLOW. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
- Handshake with PMC to take TCMs and caches out of retention mode
- PMC
- Brings RAMs out of retention modes
- AO-PM
Switch Cortex-R5 clocks and AOPLL back to their ACTIVE state sources. This step must wait for RAIL_RET exit to complete. AO-PM implements the rail retention exit timer.
- AO-CAR
Switch Cortex-R5 clocks and AOPLL to TGT_ACTIVE or TGT_ACTIVE_IRQFIQ mode. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
Upon completion of RAIL_RET exit, RAM_RET exit requests, unblock TCM accesses on AXI-S path and unblock interrupts. Do not wait for clocks and AOPLL to switch to TGT_ACTIVE mode.
- AO-PM
Upon completion of RAIL_RET exit, RAM_RET exit requests, unblock interrupt. Do not wait for clocks and AOPLL to switch to TGT_ACTIVE mode.
- AO-PM
- Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register
Set CURRENT_STATE == ACTIVE or ACTIVE_IRQFIQ.
- SPE-DORMANT Entry Sequence

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Agent
- Step
- Cortex-R5 SW
Prepare I/O controllers for power-gating mode. For each I/O controller: 1. 2. 3. 4. Execute context save operations after the controller is in idle state. Clock gate the controller. Assert reset to the controller. Switch I/O controller clocks to 32 kHz. Put the CAN message RAMs into SLEEP mode. Flush and disable the L1 caches and place them in SLEEP mode.
- Cortex-R5 SW
- Set up AO-PM with power state transition command by writing to AOPM_TGT_PWR _STATE
register.
- STATE == DORMANT
RAM_RET and/or RAIL_RET is enabled. Set up AO-CAR with power state transition info by writing to registers described in Clocks Chapter
- TGT_DORMANT_SHALLOW (should be no slower than OSC)
- TGT_DORMANT (expected to be 32 kHz)
(Can optionally set up the Rail retention Control Mode in the PMC.)
- Cortex-R5 SW
- Execute WFI
- Cortex-R5
- AO-PM
Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to TGT_DORMANT_SHALLOW. Place AO-PLL in low power mode.
- AO-CAR
Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to TGT_DORMANT_SHALLOW if enabled. Place AO-PLL in TGT_DORMANT_SHALLOW mode. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
Block accesses at the AXI-S interface. This prevents TCM accesses when the TCM is in retention. Block interrupts at the output of the AVIC. This prevents Cortex-R5 from being woken up when TCM and caches are held in retention.
- AO-PM
Put RAMs in RET mode by implementing handshake with the PMC.
- PMC
- Upon receiving request from AO-PM, the PMC PG Controller puts the AOPG RAMs in RET mode
and sends an ACK to AO-PM.
- Before switching the AO-PM clock itself to 32 kHz, the reset and PG sequence, OSC control
sequence must be completed. AO-PM first waits for the PMC to go into SC7, executes the below sequences handshaking with PMC, and then switches the clocks to 32 kHz.
- AO-PM
Wait for PMC to indicate that SC7 entry is completed.
- PMC
De-assert SC7 commit indication once SC7 entry is completed and there is no pending wake event detected for SC7 exit. Wait for AO-PM to deassert AOPM acknowledgement before responding to a subsequent SC7 action. The rest of the Dormant state entry sequence holds off SC7 wake events.
- AO-PM
Hold off PMC from exiting SC7.
- AOPM
AO-PM sends a request to NOC PM to ensure there are no outstanding transactions in NoC.
- AON-NOC
Handshakes with T-NIUs in NOC to ensure no outstanding transactions, and sends an ack back to the PM.
- AON-NOC
Handshakes with power disconnect modules in NoC and sends a second ACK to the PM.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Agent
- Step
- AO-PM
- Once idle is received from NoC, PM sequences the module resets and module clock enables in
preparation for power-gating AOPG domain. The modules that are reset in Hardware are
- Cortex-R5 resets (nSYSPORESET, PRESETDBG, DBGRESET and nRESET)
- AON VIC reset
- AON Fabric resets
1. 2. 3.
- The module that can be reset only when the system is in SC7 is the
- AON-NoC resets
1. The AO-PM waits for the PMC to go into SC7 before resetting any module for power-gating.
- AO-CAR
Assert requested resets and gate requested clocks and acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
- Place the Cortex-R5 in HALT mode until the PG exit sequence is completed
- AO-PM
Trigger PG state machine to assert clamp enables and sleep enables for AOPG domain. Wait for completion of PG entry sequence.
- AO-PM
- Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to a clock source programmed in
TGT_DORMANT_DEEP_CLKSRC and assert AOPLL low power mode.
- AO-CAR
- Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks to TGT_DORMANT_CLKSRC if enabled and place AOPLL in low
power mode defined by this configuration. Acknowledge AO-PM.
- AO-PM
Request PMC to turn OSC off.
- PMC
Conditionally turn OSC off if enabled by BPMP.
- AO-PM
- Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register
- CURRENT_STATE == DORMANT_DEEP
- SPE-DORMANT Exit Sequence
- Agent
- Step
- AO-PM
Detects exit conditions:
- Pending interrupt (in shallow state) from AVIC outputs
- Pending wake events from the Wake Engine
- SC7 indication to exit low power states
- Software triggered exit from external NoC master access (valid only until AOPG is power
gated) 1. 2. 3. 4.
- AO-PM
- Request for OSC pad to be turned on
- PMC
- Turn OSC pad on
- AO-PM
- Switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks back to TGT_DORMANT_SHALLOW (required to be OSC). This
also switches AOPLL to the corresponding mode.
- AO-PM
- Requests PMC PG state machine to get RAMs out of RET mode and AOPG domain out of power
gating.
- If the exit condition is due to an SC7 wake request from PMC, then re-enable DEEP_DORMANT
override in hardware.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Agent
- Step
- AO-PM
Once AOPG domain is unpower gated, take the modules out of reset that are not under software reset control. This sends a request to the NOC to do power-ungating steps. Then indicates to the SC7 sequencer that AON exit actions are complete. Once the rail is exited from retention voltage, take the TCMs and SRAMs out of RET mode.
- Switch AOPLL back to TGT_ACTIVE and switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks back to TGT_ACTIVE
sources. Wait for ACK from AO-CAR. Request CAR to deassert resets to all hardware-controlled modules. The modules whose resets are removed in hardware are: 1. 2. 3.
- Cortex-R5 resets (nSYSPORESET, PRESETDBG, DBGRESET and nRESET)
- AON VIC reset
- AON Fabric resets
- AO-CAR
- Switch AOPLL back to TGT_ACTIVE and switch Cortex-R5 subsystem clocks back to TGT_ACTIVE
sources. Switch PWR-I2C clock back to TGT_ACTIVE source. Send acknowledgement to AO-PM. Take modules out of reset as requested by AO-PM and send acknowledgement back to AO-PM.
- AO-PM
Once TCMs are out of RET mode, unblock the AXI-S.
- AO-PM
- Once the Cortex-R5 resets are removed and AO-CAR sends an acknowledgement back, and the
SRAMs have also been taken out of retention mode, take the Cortex-R5 out of HALT mode.
- AO-PM
- Update AOPM_PWR_STATUS register
- CURRENT_STATE == ACTIVE
##### 9.13.2.16.3 AO-CAR
The block called AO-CAR is the CAR parts that reside in the AO voltage domain. AO-CAR provides the clocks and resets to both the AON cluster and the other blocks that are inside the AO voltage domain.AO-CAR
##### 9.13.2.16.4 DMA Controller
The AON cluster DMA controller is an eight-channel implementation of the central DMA controller. The DMA request from the AON Cluster (I2C[2:0], SPI, UART, and DMIC) are connected to the local
- DMA controller. Refer to the General Purpose DMA (GPC-DMA, AON, BPMP, and SCE DMA) chapter
in this TRM for more details.
- Table 9.100 DMA Request Mapping
- Block
- TX DMA Request Port
(DMA_TX [31:0])
- RX DMA Request Port
(DMA_RX [31:0])
- Reserved
- I2C2 (AON_I2C2)
- I2C8 (AON_I2C3)
- SPI2 (AON_SPI1)

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Block
- TX DMA Request Port
(DMA_TX [31:0])
- RX DMA Request Port
(DMA_RX [31:0])
- UARTC (AON UARTC)
- UARTJ (SBSA_UART)
- DMIC
N/A
- GTE
N/A
- UART6 [from SoC for closed system debug]
- UART8 [from SoC for closed system debug]
##### 9.13.2.16.5 Address Translation
The AON Cluster is required to support sending requests to DRAM that indicate to the SMMU if the request requires translation to support a physical carve-out. The SMMU needs to handle requests differently based on if a translation is requested. Since the Cortex-R5 is not capable of indicating if a request requires translation, an Address Space Translation Engine (AST) is used to define the address regions that require translation. The AST is also used to remap the Cortex-R5 and DMA generated address to prevent address space collision for I/O virtual addresses. The AON cluster includes two eight region AST modules. Refer to Address Space Translation (AST) for more information. The AON Cluster is only allowed to bypass SMMU translation when accessing the AON Cluster-only carve-out. If a translation is not requested, the SMMU must bypass the address translation and ensure the address is inside the AON Cluster DRAM region. If a translation is requested, the SMMU performs the translation and ensures that the translated address is not in the AON Cluster region (or any other protected region).
##### 9.13.2.16.6 Shared Mailboxes
- The AON cluster uses shared MMIO mailbox registers to communicate between the Cortex-R5
other processors in the system. The mailbox implementation is defined in the Hardware
- Synchronization Primitives (HSP) chapter of this TRM. The HSP in the AON cluster uses the
following configuration i.e., eight Shared Mail Box Instances (nSM) and four Shared Semaphore (nSS) instances. The number of Shared interrupts i.e., nSI is five. nSM nAS nSS nDB nSI
- Notes
SI[0] is sent to the AVIC. [SI[4:1] are sent to the LIC.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
##### 9.13.2.16.7 Timekeeping Engine (TKE)
Cortex-R5 does not have built-in timers, the timers are integrated into the BPMP subsystem with an external Timekeeping Engine (TKE). The timekeeping unit provides the following functionalities: 1. 2. 3.
- Timers (NV_timers)
- Watchdog (WDT)
- Timestamp counter (TSC) read-back
- Table 9.101 AON TKE Configuration
- Parameter
- Value
- Number of NV_timers (nT)
- Number of watchdogs (nW)
- Interrupt routing
- Disabled
- Number of shared interrupts exposed
- TSC input type (isSerial)
1 (Serial)
**Figure 9.100 AON Timers and WDT Hookup**

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Watchdog Timer
A watchdog timer associated with the AON Cortex-R5 is instanced to monitor Cortex-R5 CPU's health. The watchdog timer has robust restart mechanisms like windowed watchdog and Question and Answer watchdog. AON cluster has a dedicated watchdog timer instanced in the cluster. This enables us to architect the functionality of this timer as per AON clusters specific requirements.
##### 9.13.2.16.8 Timestamping (GTE)
- The AON cluster implements a timestamp module (GTE) for logging the current TSC value when
interrupts or GPIO events occur.
##### 9.13.2.16.9 GTE and DMA
- The AON DMA engine can be used to read from the GTE timestamp FIFO instead of reading the
FIFO using the Cortex-R5. One use case is for the GTE to timestamp the GPIO changes to recover a waveform (for example handling IR receive). To use GTE and DMA in this manner, the following configuration must be used. GTE:
- GTE_GPIO_TECTRL_0[AUTOADVADDR] = 0x3
- GTE_GPIO_TECTRL_0[OCCUPANCYTHRESHOLD] = 0x1
- GTE_GPIO_TECTRL_0[INTERRUPTENABLE] = 0x1
DMA:
- GPCDMA_FLV_8CH_NON_SAFE_CHANNEL_CH<X>_MMIO_SEQ_0[MMIO_BURST] = 0x7
- GPCDMA_FLV_8CH_NON_SAFE_CHANNEL_CH<X>_MMIO_SEQ_0[MMIO_ADDR_WRAP] = 0x4
- GPCDMA_FLV_8CH_NON_SAFE_CHANNEL_CH<X>_SRC_PTR_0[SRC_PTR] = GTE base
address
- GPCDMA_FLV_8CH_NON_SAFE_CHANNEL_CH<X>_MC_SEQ_0[MC_BURST] = 0x0
- GPCDMA_FLV_8CH_NON_SAFE_CHANNEL_CH<X>_DMA_BYTE_TRA_0[TRANSFER_COUNT] =
8x4x (number of samples) When using this configuration, the GTE interrupt in the AVIC must be masked.
##### 9.13.2.16.10 I2C
The AON cluster implements two I2C controllers.

- AON Cluster Functional Description
- Table 9.102 AON I2C Controllers
- Global AMAP Name
- Key Usage Examples
- I2C2
- Camera configuration, Touch control, Sensor interfaces
- I2C8
- Display Aggregator, Sensor Hub Control
##### 9.13.2.16.11 SPI
The AON cluster implements one SPI controller.
- Table 9.103 AON SPI Controller
- AON SPI Instance
- Global AMAP Name
- AON_SPI
- SPI2
##### 9.13.2.16.12 UART
The AON cluster implements two UARTs for primary debug purposes. UARTC is an instance of the standard UART IP, but is implemented as a two pin UART with the CTS and RTS pins tied off so the IP can always send and receive data. UARTJ is the SBSA Compliant UART for debug purposes.
- See Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter (UART)
- Table 9.104 AON UARTs
- Global AMAP Name
- UART Type
- Usage Details
- UARTC
2pin
- Primary Two Pin Debug UART. Can also be controlled by
- BPMP and CCPLEX with interrupts routed to BPMP cluster
and LIC. This is the case for flow control signals also, i.e., routed to BPMP Cluster DMA and GPC DMA.
- UART_J
2 pin SBSA Compliant UART.
##### 9.13.2.16.13 PWM
- The AON cluster implements one PWM. The details of the PMW can be found in the Pulse Width
Modulator (PWM) chapter of this document.
##### 9.13.2.16.14 GPIO Controllers
- The AON cluster implements a GPIO controller and pinmux module. Both modules have separate
APB interfaces. There are four interrupts (two functional and two time-stamp interrupts) from the GPIO controller. One functional interrupt and one timestamp interrupt is sent from the GPIO block to the AON interrupt controller. All functional and timestamp interrupts is sent to LIC.

- AON Programming Guidelines
##### 9.13.2.16.15 DMIC
The AON cluster implements a DMIC controller for supporting voice wake functionality. The AON DMIC controller is optimized for low power voice wake requirements.
##### 9.13.2.16.16 CEC
The Consumer Electronic Controller (CEC) unit is added in the AO domain. The motivation for adding the controller to AON is to be able to wake the system up from SC7 only on messages indicated to the SoC and not to wake up on unintended or spurious toggles of the input (wake capable pad).
### 9.13.3 AON Programming Guidelines
#### 9.13.3.1 Power Management
##### 9.13.3.1.1 Cluster Power State Definitions
The AO Power State framework consists of SPE power states on one hand and the cluster's activity levels mapped to System Power States on the other. The SPE subsystem in the AO cluster is in one of four power states: 1. 2. 3. 4.
- SPE-Active
- SPE-Idle
- SPE-Standby
- SPE-Dormant

- AON Programming Guidelines
**Figure 9.101 SPE Power States Hierarchy within System Power States Framework**
These power states are defined by the sensor processor and sensor I/Os expected activity levels. The system and power management functions in the AO cluster operate at various levels of activity but are never switched off completely except when the SoC is fully powered off (shut down). At the cluster level there is no specific idle or standby state defined. The AO cluster is an always-on domain in the chip. In any of the SoC power states, the AO cluster could be in any of its own power states. However, there are some constraints on the AO cluster state while the chip transitions into and out of the SC7 SoC states.

- AON Programming Guidelines
**Figure 9.102 AO Rail Power States Transition Framework**
In the steady state, any of the AO cluster power states (except complete power-off) are supported by hardware in any of the SoC power states.
##### 9.13.3.1.2 Cortex-R5 CPU Halted (Clock Gating)
If the CPU is idle for a short period of time during WFI state, then it can be halted which results in its CPU clock-gating. The WFI instruction is executed for putting CPU into halt. After executing WFI, CPU disables most of the clocks in the CPU except the clock for the small wake up logic clock. Exit from halt state occurs when the CPU detects an interrupt on IRQ or FIQ pins.
##### 9.13.3.1.3 TCM Clock Gating
The TCM interface clock can be gated when there are no pending DMA requests and the Cortex-R5 is halted. The nCLKSTOPPED signal is used to indicate when the TCM clock can be gated. The TCM clock must be restarted in no more than four clocks after nCLKSTOPPED is deasserted.
##### 9.13.3.1.4 OSC Power Down
To reduce SC7 power when the AON Cluster is idle for an extended period (greater than 3 ms), all AON Cluster clocks except for the 32 kHz clock can be disabled by powering down the OSC pad. When the OSC is powered down, only the RTC, or an external event, can wake the AON Cluster.

- AON Programming Guidelines
##### 9.13.3.1.5 Software Enable Disable of AON PLL
The AON cluster clocking logic does not allow the AON Cortex-R5 to directly turn on the AON cluster PLL. When the SoC is not in SC7, the AON Cortex-R5 can make a request to BPMP to enable or disable the PLL (normally the AON PLL is on when the SoC is on). When the SoC is in SC7, the BPMP is not powered. If the AON Cortex-R5 requires the PLL to turn on and off in SC7, the Cortex-
- R5 firmware must use the ACTIVE and ACTIVE_IRQFIQ power states defined in AOPM. The AON
Cortex-R5 can use these two states to define a state with the PLL on (ACTIVE_IRQFIQ) and a state with the PLL off (ACTIVE). The following conditions must be met for AON firmware to control the PLL state in SC7. No I/O controllers can be configured to use a clock from the AON PLL.
- The BPMP must configure the ACTIVE_IRQFIQ and ACTIVE clock settings prior to entering
SC7.
- Prior to executing WFI firmware must ensure that any previous software request is complete
by a. b. [AON-R5-FW] Read AON_SW_TGT_REQ. If 1, go to Step 2, else go to Step 4. [AON-R5-FW] Poll AON_SW_TGT_ACK until it is 1 in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register. [AON-R5-FW] Program AON_SW_TGT_REQ to 0 in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register. [AON-R5-FW] Poll AON_SW_TGT_ACK in the
- CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register until it is set to 0 by
hardware. c. d.
- ACTIVE to ACTIVE_IRQFIQ Firmware Sequence
Entering the ACTIVE_IRQFIQ state typically requires an interrupt to be triggered. To prevent the
- PLL from being turned on every time an interrupt occurs, the ACTIVE_IRQFIQ state should be
disabled: (AOPM_BURST_CLK_0[BURST_CLK_EN]= 0 and AOPM_BURST_CLK_0[BURST_LP_EN]= 0). 1. 2. [AON-R5-FW] Read AON_SW_TGT_REQ. If 1, go to Step 2, else go to Step 4. [AON-R5-FW] Poll AON_SW_TGT_ACK until it is 1 in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register. [AON-R5-FW] Program AON_SW_TGT_REQ to 0 in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register. [AON-R5-FW] Poll AON_SW_TGT_ACK in the
- CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register until it is set to 0 by
hardware. [AON-R5-FW] Program the AON_SW_TGT_STATE field in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register to 0x1. 3. 4. 5.

- AON Programming Guidelines
6. [AON-R5-FW] Program the AON_SW_TGT_REQ bit in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register to 1. [CAR HW] Transition to ACTIVE_IRQFIQ state and set AON_SW_TGT_ACK when complete. 7.
- ACTIVE_IRQFIQ to ACTIVE Sequence
1. 2. [AON-R5-FW] Read AON_SW_TGT_REQ. If 1, go to Step 2, else go to Step 4. [AON-R5-FW] Poll AON_SW_TGT_ACK until it is 1 in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register. [AON-R5-FW] Program AON_SW_TGT_REQ to 0 in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register. [AON-R5-FW] Poll AON_SW_TGT_ACK in the
- CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register until it is set to 0 by
hardware. [AON-R5-FW] Program the AON_SW_TGT_STATE field in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register to 0x0. [AON-R5-FW] Program the AON_SW_TGT_REQ bit in the CLK_RST_CONTROLLER_AON_SEQ_SW_INTERFACE_CTL_0 register to 1. [CAR HW] Transition to ACTIVE state and set AON_SW_TGT_ACK when complete. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7.
##### 9.13.3.1.6 AON Cluster Power Gating
The AON cluster supports power gating most of the units within the cluster to minimize the AON power to meet the Automotive CAN standby power targets. PMC also has control register for power gating the Cortex-R5 cache RAMs, the BTCM RAMs, and the CAN controller RAMs. The figure below shows the expected PG control connections in AON.

- AON Programming Guidelines
**Figure 9.103 NPG vs PG view of AON IPs**
- Gating Entry Sequence
1. [AON-R5-FW] Detects an idle condition when system in SC7 that can tolerate greater than 10 ms of wake time. [AON-R5-FW] Enables wake detection on for AON external wake sources in PMC. [AON-R5-FW] Asserts Reset to each power gateable I/O controller. [AON-R5-FW] Stops first level clocks to each power gateable I/O controller. [AON-R5-FW] Save any required core logic state (Cortex-R5, AVIC, TKE, GTE, HSP) state. [AON-R5-FW] Asserts reset to TKE, GTE, HSP. [AON-R5-FW] Stops first level clocks to TKE, GTE, HSP. [AON-R5-FW] Sets the target power state to DORMANT in AO-PM. [AON-R5-FW] Executes WFI which triggers power gating for the AO cluster logic including the CAN controllers. [PMC] Waits for external wake event. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7. 8. 9. 10.

- AON Programming Guidelines
- Power Gating Exit Sequence
1. 2. 3. 4. [PMC] Wake received and triggers AO-PM. [AO-PM] Restores AO Cluster to Active state. [AON-R5-FW] Enable first level clocks to TKE, GTE, HSP. [AON-R5-FW] Deasserts reset to TKE, GTE, HSP. a. b. Reset to TKE should be removed after GTE reset if GTE time stamping is used.
- See the Timers chapter for details on resyncing the GTE and TKE with the TSC after exiting
PG. 5. 6. 7. 8. [AON-R5-FW] Restore required state to core logic. [AON-R5-FW] Enables first level clocks to each power required I/O controllers. [AON-R5-FW] Deasserts Reset to each required I/O controller. [AON-R5-FW] Initialize required I/O controllers for normal operation.
- Cortex-R5 Cache Power Gating
When the system enters SC7, the AON Cortex-R5 can no longer access DRAM. Prior to SC7 entry the AON Cortex-R5 must flush and disable its caches. To save leakage the Cortex-R5 caches can be power-gated after the caches are disabled.
- Power Gating Entry Sequence
1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7. [SW-AP] Decides to go into SC7. [SW-AP] Notifies AON Cluster firmware of SC7 Entry. [FW-AON R5] Flushes L1 data cache and write buffers. [FW-AON R5] Disables the instruction and data caches. [FW-AON R5] Programs AON Cortex-R5 Cache Power-gating control register in PMC. [HW-PMC] Power gates Cortex-R5 cache by asserting the cache SD pins. [FW-AON R5] Notifies AP/BPMP that it is safe to enter SC7.
- Power Gating Exit Sequence
1. 2. 3. 4. 5. [FW-BPMP] Notifies AON Cluster firmware that system is out of SC7. [FW-AON R5] Programs AON Cortex-R5 Cache Power-gating control register in PMC. [HW-PMC] Deasserts the SD pins to the Cortex-R5 cache RAMs. [FW-AON R5] Enables the instruction and data caches. [FW-AON R5] Performs invalidate all to initialize the instruction and data caches.
- BTCM Power Gating
The BTCM for the AON Cortex-R5 can enter power gating by: 1. 2.
- Software initiated RAM Shutdown
- Hardware initiated RAM Sleep or Deep Sleep

- AON Programming Guidelines
3.
- Software initiated RAM Sleep or Deep Sleep
- BTCM RAM Shutdown
Software initiated RAM shutdown is controlled from PMC. PMC contains one power-gating control register for each BTCM bank. When a bank is placed in SD, the BTCM logic must return an error is the bank is accessed. Software is responsible for saving any data in the TCM bank to DRAM if state needs to be maintained.
- Power Gating Entry Sequence
1. 2. 3. 4. [FW-AON R5] Decides to go put TCM bank in SD [FW-AON R5] Copies any required data to DRAM. [FW-AON R5] Programs AON TCM Bank N Power-gating control register in PMC. [HW-PMC] Power gates Cortex-R5 cache by asserting the cache SD pins.
- Power Gating Exit Sequence
1. 2. 3. 4. [FW-AON R5] Determines a TCM Bank needs to be enabled [FW-AON R5] Programs AON TCM Bank N Power gating control register in PMC. [HW-PMC] Deasserts the SD pins to the Cortex-R5 cache RAMs [FW-AON R5] Restores TCM data from DRAM if required.
- CAN Power Gating
The two CAN controllers are power gated at the same time the AON cluster core logic is power gated.
- CAN Power Gating Entry Sequence
1. [CAN-SW] Detects idle condition (involves detecting idle condition of network and controller) and system in SC7. [CAN-SW] Puts external Transceiver in Low power (or sleep or listen only) mode. [CAN-SW] Enables Wake detection on CAN WAKE (can be Rx pad for some transceivers) Pad in PMC. [CAN-SW] Asserts Reset to CAN controller. [CAN-SW] Stop CAN[1:2] first Level Clocks. [CAN-SW] Writes to the CAN[1:2] Power-gating control registers in PMC to power gate the CAN message RAM. [CAN-SW] Save any required Cortex-R5 and AVIC state. [CAN-SW] Sets the target power state to DORMANT in AO-PM. [CAN-SW] Executes WFI which triggers power gating for the AO cluster logic including the CAN controllers. [PMC] Waits for Wake event from CAN Network. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7. 8. 9. 10.
- CAN Power Gating Exit Sequence

- AON Programming Guidelines
1. 2. 3. 4. [PMC] Wake received and triggers AO-PM. [AO-PM] Restores AO Cluster to Active state. [CAN-SW] Restore required state to Cortex-R5 and AVIC. [CAN-SW] Writes to the CAN[1:2] Power-gating control registers in PMC to ungate the CAN message RAM. [CAN-SW] Enable Clocks. [CAN-SW] Deassert reset. [CAN-SW] Disable Wake on CAN receive pad. [CAN-SW] Bring transceiver out of low power mode. [CAN-SW] Initialize controllers for normal operation. 5. 6. 7. 8. 9.
#### 9.13.3.2 AON Cluster SC7 Entry
Access from the AON cluster to the SOC is blocked by the software when entering SC7. If requests are in flight when the block logic is engaged it is possible a deadlock could occur. Software should follow the sequence below to ensure this deadlock does not happen. 1. 2.
- BPMP notifies AON FW to stop all SOC access (MMIO and DRAM) via an IPC message
- AON firmware performs all necessary steps to be able to operate with no SOC access. (Swap
code into TCM, flush, and Disable caches, etc.) After this point AON firmware is executing only from TCM.
- AON firmware sends IPC to BPMP that it is safe to disable SOC access
BPMP acknowledges IPC by writing to HSP mailbox in AON. AON FW detects AON mailbox write and acknowledges by writing to AON HSP.
- BPMP polls AON HSP waiting for the acknowledgment from AON. When the acknowledgment
is received, PSC writes to AOPM to enable the block function. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7.
#### 9.13.3.3 AON Cortex-R5 Activity Monitor
The unit activity monitor is a part of the AO-PM module. The interrupt from the unit ACTMON is not merged with the AO-PM interrupt. It is separately routed to the local VIC and to LIC.
- AON uses a type-1 unit ACTMON [refer to the Activity Monitor (ACTMON) chapter of this
document] to detect idle periods of the processor complex and monitor a few key events that the Cortex-R5 exposes on its event bus. The idle state of the Cortex-R5 is not exposed to the event bus. Therefore, the idle signals nCLKSTOPPED, nWFIPIPESTOPPED, and nWFEPIPESTOPPED must be separately exported to detect idle period statistics and use the information for scaling the performance of the Cortex-R5. The outputs from the Cortex-R5 are sampled at the processor clock frequency and a cumulative counter incremented for every cycle that the unit_active signal is asserted. The cumulative count is

- AON Programming Guidelines
compared with a target count. When the cumulative count in processor clock rate reaches the target value, the ACTMON is notified. The EVENTBUS signals hooked up to the ACTMON are indicative of the following events.
- EVENTBUS[5]: Indicates that the instruction is architecturally executed. Asserted on every
cycle when an instruction was architecturally executed by the processor.
- EVENTBUS[16]: Stalls because instruction buffer cannot deliver an instruction. This can
indicate an instruction cache miss. Asserted every cycle where the condition is present.
- EVENTBUS[19]: External memory request. Examples of this are cache refill, non-cacheable
accesses, write-through writes, cache line evictions (write-back).
- EVENTBUS[20]: Stalls because of Load Store Unit (LSU) being busy. The event takes place
each clock cycle where the condition is met. A high incidence of this event indicates the pipeline is often waiting for transactions to complete on the external bus. The unit ACTMON uses aon_cpu_clk as the refclk.
#### 9.13.3.4 Reset and Clocking
##### 9.13.3.4.1 Reset
There are several independent reset domains in the AON Cluster to be able to reset independently different parts of the Cortex-R5 and AON Cluster.
- Cortex-R5 Reset
Cortex-R5 can start executing instructions as soon as its reset is released (deasserted). Hence, it is important to ensure that the entire system is ready to accept instructions before Cortex-R5 is released out of reset.
- Table 9.105 Cortex-R5 Reset Domains
- Reset Domain
- Description
- ACPRESETn
- Since Cortex-R5 subsystem doesn't have an ACP, this is tied inactive (to 1)
nRESET CPU non-debug logic reset.
- Notes: Whenever nRESET is asserted, it should be asserted for at least four
CLKIN cycles for correct reset operation.
- This is the signal that is pulsed by the watchdog timer on a "processor hang"
if debug through reset is needed. nSYSPORESET
- Cortex-R5 system reset
This resets the Cortex-R5 processor and debug logic.
- Note: Whenever nSYSPORESET is asserted, it should be asserted for at least
four CLKIN cycles for correct reset operation. nPRESETDBG Cortex-R5 Debug reset. Resets debug-domain debug logic and the APB interface of the CPU.

- AON Registers
- Reset Domain
- Description
nCPUHALT Prevents the Cortex-R5 from fetching code out of reset.
- DBGRESETn
Cortex-R5 Debug reset.
- Resets core-domain debug logic. This includes breakpoints, watchpoints, and
the DCC registers.
- AON Cluster Reset
There are four groups of resets in the AON cluster as shown in the table below. They are grouped so that the reset assertion and deassertion can be understood in terms of groups rather than a bunch of independent signals.
- Table 9.106 Reset Groups in AON Cluster
- Group 0
- Group 1
- Group 2
- Group 3
- System reset + All AON cluster
resets, with the exception of Groups 1, 2, and 3 resets
- CAN1 and CAN2 reset
nSYSPORRESET, VIC0, VIC1 nCPUHALT The order of deassertion of resets on cold boot is from Group 0 through Group 3. 1. Group 0 reset is the system reset. This reset is the system reset and is generated by PMC. This reset is the reset for most modules in the AON cluster. Group 1 reset is the Group 0 reset qualified by the fuses valid signal, straps valid signal and
- RAM valid signal. This reset goes to the CAN controllers. DBGRESETn, nPRESETDBG, and
nRESET may be extended up to four clocks after the Group 1 reset deasserts. Group 2 reset group is primarily the Cortex-R5 Power-on-Reset and the PL192 resets. This is the last module in the AON cluster that comes out of reset. The rest of the cluster is ready to receive and process Cortex-R5 CPU's requests as soon as Cortex-R5 is released. The Group 2 reset is the same as Group 1 reset except it may be extended up to four clocks.
- Group 3 reset group includes the nCPUHALT. This isn't truly a processor reset, but can be
used to hold processor from executing instructions. For the AON cluster nCPUHALT should remain asserted until firmware has been loaded into the TCM. To do this as FWLoadDone bit is set by the boot code once the TCM load is complete. The FWLoadDone bit is not cleared except by an aon_cluster_reset. 2. 3. 4.

of AON_AST_0 and AON_AST_1. For details of the registers descriptions and base addresses, refer to the Address Space Translator (AST) Registers in the Address Space Translation (AST) chapter.

- TKE_AON_SHARED_TKECR_0
- TKE Control register
- Offset: 0x10
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: TKESCR_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 HDBG: Halt-on-debug. Controls whether a Halt-on-debug signal halts TKE logic. 0: TKE ignores Halt-on-debug. 1: Asserted Halt-on-debug signal halts TKE logic, by disabling the update of the reference counters and reference timing signals for all timers and all WDTs 0x0
- OSC_DIS: Disable the local OSC counter. Can be used to decrease the power
consumption if OSC is not required either by hardware as a reference clock or by software 0x0
- USEC_DIS: Disable the local USEC counter. Can be used to decrease the power
consumption if USEC is not required either by hardware as a reference clock or by software 0x0
- TSC_DIS: Disable the local TSC. Can be used to decrease the power
consumption if TSC is not required either by hardware as a reference clock or by software
- TKE_AON_SHARED_CLK_OVR_ON_0
- Offset: 0x14

- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxx0)

- AON Registers

- Reset

0x0
- OSC: SLCG override bit
0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE
- TKE_AON_TIMER_TMR0_TMRCR_0
- Offset: 0x10000
Read/Write: See table below.

- SCR Protection: TMRSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x40000000 (0b01x0,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 EN: Enable, when set, enables the countdown process. 0x1
- PER: Periodic, when set, the internal counter is automatically reloaded after
reaching 0 28:0 0x0
- PTV: Timer Present Trigger Value. When TMRCR is written and EN is 1b, the
internal counter is loaded with PTV+1 and starts counting down at each timing reference pulse. An interrupt is set when the internal counter reaches 0. Note that writing TMRCR with EN = 1 while EN was already 1 restarts the counter.
- Once the timer is started, TARGET can be used to either observe the value of
the reference counter when the next interrupt is generated (read) or select the exact reference counter value at which the next interrupt is generated (write)

- AON Registers
- TKE_AON_TIMER_TMR3_TMRATR_0
- Timer Absolute Target Register
- Offset: 0x4000c
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: TMRSCR3_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxx00,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
29:0 0x0
- TARGET: Target value of the selected reference counter, i.e. when value of the
selected reference equals target value, the interrupt is generated. The difference between TARGET and the current value must be positive when interpreting TARGET and the 29 LSB of the selected reference counter as signed integers, otherwise the interrupt triggers immediately.
- TKE_AON_WDT0_WDTCR_0
- Watchdog Timer Configuration Register
- Offset: 0x50000
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: WDTSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x00700000 (0b0000,xx00,0111,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:28 0x0
- WindowedRestartDisableMap: When Windowed Operation Enable is 1b,
restart commands attempted when expiration level has value b are rejected if bit b equals 1b in this field 0x0
- DisallowWDTFreeze: When 1b, the signal watchdog_freeze_request has no
effect When 0b, when the signal watchdog_freeze_request is asserted, the
- WDT stops decrementing Current Count if the WDT is enabled
24:23 0x0
- RestartErrorAction: If a restart action is attempted but fails, indicate which
of 4 actions should be taken. 0x0: do nothing, silently ignore the restart 0x1: immediately jump to expiration level 4 and reload the downcounter. 0x2: immediately jump to expiration level 5 and reload the downcounter. 0x3: immediately request a system power-on-reset When jumping levels, the normal actions configured per level must take place 0 = NOP 1 = JUMP_TO_4 2 = JUMP_TO_5 3 = POR_RESET 22:20 0x7
- ErrorThreshold: The WDT logic asserts an error signal to HSM when
ExpirationLevel >= ErrorThreshold. 0x0
- TscReferenceEnable: Select as timing reference transitions on a configured
TSC bit.

- AON Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0
- ChallengeResponseEnable: Enable the Challenge Response mode of
operation. When 1b, the Unlock register operates as a Challenge Response register. 0x0
- WindowedOperationEnable: Enable the windowed mode of operation. When
1b a restart command is rejected when the current expiration level is identified as disallowed in the WindowedRestartDisableMap or the current time is below the window threshold. The rejection is either silent or perform a reset based on RestartErrorAction 0x0
- SystemPOResetEnable: Enable internal full system reset at fifth expiration of
the counter. This reset cannot be intercepted and acts as a Power On Reset 0x0
- SystemDebugResetEnable: Enable system wide reset assertion at fourth
expiration of the counter. This reset can be intercepted by debug logic, e.g., to preserve information 0x0
- RemoteInterruptEnable: Enable normal priority interrupt assertion at third
expiration of the counter, connected to LIC for system wide visibility 0x0
- LocalFIQEnable: Enable high priority interrupt (FIQ) assertion at second
expiration of the counter, connected to local interrupt controller 0x0
- LocalInterruptEnable: Enable normal priority interrupt assertion at first
expiration of the counter, connected to local interrupt controller 11:4 0x0
- Period: Measured in periods of the timer selected as source, this is the reload
value, so 0 is treated as maximum period 3:0 0x0
- TimerSource: The timer source interpretation is dependent on the value of
- TscReferenceEnable: TscReferenceEnable = 0b: timer<TimerSource> is the
timing reference TscReferenceEnable = 1b: a transition of
- TSC[2*TimerSource] is the timing reference
- TKE_AON_WDT0_WDTSR_0
- Watchdog Timer Status Register
- Offset: 0x50004
- Read/Write: RO
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: WDTSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,0000,x000,0000,0000,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0
- Frozen: 1b indicates that the WDT is frozen because of the assertion of the
global WDT freeze request (when allowed) or because of the assertion of the global timer freeze request 0x0
- ErrorForced: 1b indicates that the error reported to HSM is forced active
for testing purpose 0x0
- StickyEnabled: 1b when the counter is enabled and cannot be disabled, set
by StickyStart command, cleared by reset only.

- AON Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0
- CurrentError: Current error reported to HSM, this is the output after
combining the functional and forced error paths 14:12 0x0
- CurrentExpirationCount: Current count of expiration since last start
operation, cleared by Start command, saturating at 5. 11:4 0x0
- CurrentCount: Current value of the downcounter, reloaded with Period by
Start command. 0x0
- RemoteInterruptStatus: Current status of remote interrupt, cleared by
- Start command
0x0 LocalFIQStatus: Current status of FIQ, cleared by Start command. 0x0
- LocalInterruptStatus: Current status of local interrupt, cleared by Start
command. 0x0
- Enabled: 1b when the counter is active. When Enabled is 1b, the
configuration of the WDT is frozen by silently ignoring write attempts to the configuration registers: WDTCR, WDTSCR and WDTFWCR. Set by Start or StickyStart commands, cleared by Disable when StickyEnabled is 0b.
- TKE_AON_WDT0_WDTCMDR_0
- Watchdog Timer Command Register
- Offset: 0x50008
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: WDTSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0
- ForceError: Only working if the Command register is unlocked. 1b sets an internal
flop that is ORED with the functional error signal (based on threshold), 0b clears the bit. The state of the flop is shown in the status register. 0x0
- StickyStart: Qualifies the StartCounter bit, if both bits are 1b, the counter is
enabled in permanent fashion, i.e., it remains enabled until the next reset, and a disable counter operation has no effect. Ignored if StartCounter is 0b 0x0
- DisableCounter: Only working if the unlock register (WDTUR{w}) has been
programmed before with the correct pattern. Writing to the command register always clears the unlock register. If written to 1b, while StartCounter is 0b and the unlock register contains the unlock pattern, the watchdog transitions back to disabled. 0x0
- StartCounter: When written to 1b, enable the counter operation, load the counter
with Period and starts downcounting, resets the expiration count to 0 and clears all status flags. Enabled is always 1b after this. This is also used as Restart. Writing to StartCounter requires unlocking when ChallengeResponseEnable is 1b, and writing to StartCounter is also restricted to windows of time given by WindowedRestartDisableMap when WindowedOperationEnable is 1b.
- TKE_AON_WDT0_WDTUR_0

- AON Registers
- Watchdog Timer Unlock Register
- Offset: 0x5000c
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: WDTSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)00000
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:0 0x0
- UnlockPattern: The behavior of the Unlock pattern is dependent on the
- ChallengeResponseEnable configuration bit When ChallengeResponseEnable is
0b, Unlock must be written with value 0x0000_C45A to allow a write to
- DisableCounter to take effect. The UnlockPattern is reset at each write to the
- Command register. When ChallengeResponseEnable is 1b, a write to the
- Command register is only allowed after reading the Unlock pattern to get the
current value of a LFSR, followed by writing the next value of the LFSR to the
- UnlockPattern. If the two values match, the unlock is successful, in all cases the
LFSR gets (re)initialized with the written value.
- TKE_AON_WDT0_WDTSCR_0
- Watchdog Timer Skip Configuration Register
- Offset: 0x50010
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: WDTSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,x000,x000,x000,x000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
14:12 0x0
- Skip3: Skip Value at Expiration count 3 At a Downcounter expiration,
the expiration level changes as ExpC <= ExpC + 1 + Skip(ExpC), saturating at 5 10:8 0x0
- Skip2: Skip Value at Expiration count 2
6:4 0x0
- Skip1: Skip Value at Expiration count 1
2:0 0x0
- Skip0: Skip Value at Expiration count 0
- TKE_AON_WDT0_WDTFWCR_0
- Watchdog Timer Fine Window Configuration Register
- Offset: 0x50014
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: WDTSCR0_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,x000,xxxx,xxxx,0000,0000)

- AON Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
18:16 0x0
- StartLevel: If restart (i.e., start while WDT is already enabled) is attempted
and windowing operation is enabled, restart is rejected if current expiration < StartLevel 7:0 0x0
- StartCount: If restart (i.e., start while WDT is already enabled) is attempted
and windowing operation is enabled and current expiration level ==
- StartLevel, restart is rejected if current downcounter > (Period -
- StartCount). StartCount is the number of cycles where rejection takes place
at the start of that level.

#### 9.13.4.2 DMAAPB
- DMAAPB_AON_ORDER_CONFIG_0
- Offset: 0x0
Read/Write: See table below.

- AON Registers
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: BR_SCR_0
- Reset: 0x00000031 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xx11,0001)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x1
- CH1_EN: Channel 1 Enable 0x0: Channel 1 is disabled. All DMA traffic go to
Channel 2 0x1: Channel 1 is enabled. 0x1
- VQMC: VQC_mux_control 0x0: Toggle muxing between channel 0, 1 and 2 (RR
arbitration) 0x1: The lowest channel number has the higher priority always. 0x0
- WRRDARB: WR_RD_ARB 0x0: RD has higher priority 0x1: WR has higher
priority 0x0
- BER: Bypass_ERR_Response. Touching of this register bits should be avoided
always. These can lead to unprecedented RTL Behavior 0x0: Use REQ rdy 0x1:
- Bypass error response
0x0 DRE: Dev_Rdy_Enable. Touching of this register bits should be avoided always.
- These can lead to unprecedented RTL Behavior 0x0: ignore DEV ack 0x1: use
- DEV ack to track responses
0x1
- BEAD: BRIDGE_EARLY_ACK_DISABLE 0x0: enable 0x1: disable
- DMAAPB_AON_ERROR_CONFIG_0
- Offset: 0x2c4
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: BR_SCR_0
- Reset: 0x00000005 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxx0,0101)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0
- ERD: Error response disable 0x0: enabled 0x1: disabled: Normal response (no
slave or decode error) is generated in case of an error Read data pattern is 0xdead_1003 for no error response 0x0
- AEC: Alignment error checking 0x0: enabled 0x1: disabled Read data pattern is
0xdead_1004 0x1
- SLEC: Size/Length error checking 0x0: Do not generate error on multi-beat
transactions of 1Byte and 2Byte size, Check Disabled 0x1: Generate error on multi-beat transactions of 1Byte and 2Byte size, Check Enabled Read data pattern is 0xdead_1005 0x0
- FTC: FIXED type support 0x0: Fixed type transactions enabled, check disabled
0x1: Fixed type transactions disabled, check enabled Read data pattern is 0xdead_1006 0x1
- WTC: WRAP type support Touching of this register bits should be avoided
always. These can lead to unprecedented RTL Behavior 0x0: enabled 0x1: disabled Read data pattern is 0xdead_1007

- CH2RFIFOF: Ch2 Request FIFO Full interrupt status. Touching of this register bit
should be avoided always for sapb_3. These can lead to unprecedented RTL
- Behavior Writing a '1' clears interrupt. Writing a '0' has no effect: 0 = no
interrupt 1= interrupt 0x0

interrupt. Writing a '0' has no effect: 0 = no interrupt 1= interrupt 0x0

interrupt. Writing a '0' has no effect: 0 = no interrupt 1= interrupt 0x0

- Reset

0x0
- SLEC1B2B_ENABLE: Bit for Size/Length error checking - AXI2APB
only supports single beat transactions of 1Byte and 2Byte size. This is for diagnostic purposes only and should not normally be changed. 0 - OFF: Generate error on multi-beat transactions of 1Byte and 2Byte size. 1 - ON: Do not Generate error on multi-beat transactions of 1Byte and 2Byte size. 0x1
- FC_ENABLE_CH3: Bit for FC This is for diagnostic purposes only and
should not normally be changed. 0 - OFF, FC Sideband Enabled 1 - ON, FC Sideband Disabled 0x1
- FC_ENABLE_CH2: Bit for FC This is for diagnostic purposes only and
should not normally be changed. 0 - OFF, FC Sideband Enabled 1 - ON, FC Sideband Disabled 0x0
- FC_ENABLE_CH1: Bit for FC This is for diagnostic purposes only and
should not normally be changed. 0 - OFF, FC Sideband Enabled 1 - ON, FC Sideband Disabled 0x1
- FC_ENABLE_CH0: Bit for FC This is for diagnostic purposes only and
should not normally be changed. 0 - OFF, FC Sideband Enabled 1 - ON, FC Sideband Disabled 0x0
- VC_ENABLE: Bit for VC This is for diagnostic purposes only and
should not normally be changed. 0 - OFF, VC Enabled 1 - ON, VC Disabled

#### 9.13.4.3 APS_AST
- APS_AST_CONTROL_0
- Offset: 0x0
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: AST_GBL_SEC_CONTROL_0
- Reset: 0x1fc80000 (0b0001,1111,11x0,1000,0xxx,xx00,000x,x000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ApbOvrOn:
- RW: APB Clock Override: Set to 1 to force apb clock always on in AST (1=
- APB SLCG is disabled)
0x0 NicOvrOn:
- RW: NIC Clock Override: Set to 1 to force nic clock always on in AST(1= NIC
- SLCG is disabled)

- AON Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
29:22 0x7f PhysStreamID:
- RWGL:Physical StreamID: This field specifies the StreamID output when
the physical stream Is is enabled for a region. 0x0 CarveOutLock:
- RW1:Carveout Lock: This bit prevents writes to all Carve Out controls when
set to 1. 0 = FALSE 1 = TRUE 0x1 DefPhysical:
- RWGL:Default Physical Select: Specifies the default how the StreamID is
selected for default accesses. 0=DefVMIndx is used to select the
- StreamID, 1 = PhysStreamID is used
18:15 0x0 DefVMIndex:
- RWGL:Default VM Index: Specifies the default VM Index used to select the
- Stream ID when DefPhysical=0
9:5 0x0 DefCarveOutID:
- RWCL:Default MC Carveout ID: Specifies the carveout ID for default
accesses. This field specifies the state output on ast_master_a[w,r]user[15,11] for requests that do not match a region. 0x0 DefSnoop: RWGL: Snoop: Specifies if default accesses snoop the Main CPU caches.
- This bit controls the state output on ast_master_a[w,r]user[8] for requests
that do not match a region. 1=Snoop request, 0=Do not snoop request 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 MatchErrCtl:
- RW:Match Error Control: Specifies how transactions that do not match a
region are handled. 0=Transactions that do no match a region are forwarded untranslated with the default attributes. 1=Transactions that do no match a region return a decode error on the AXI slave interface. 0 = NO_DECERR 1 = DECERR 0x0 Lock: RW1:Security lock: This bit prevents writes to all RWGL bits. 0 = FALSE 1 = TRUE
- APS_AST_ERROR_STATUS_0
- Offset: 0x4
- Read/Write: See table below
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: AST_GBL_SEC_CONTROL_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,x000)

- AON Registers
- Bit
R/W
- Reset
- Description
- RO
0x0 Overflow:
- RO:Error Overflow: This bit is set to 1 by HW when
- AST_ERROR_STATUS[Valid]=1 (due to a previous error) and a new
DEC_ERR occurs.
- RO
0x0 VMIndxErr:
- RO:VM Index Error: This bit is set to 1 by HW when
- AST_ERROR_STATUS[Valid]=0 and when a DEC_ERR response is
returned because a disabled VMIndx was programmed.
- RW
0x0 Valid:
- RW:Error valid: This bit is set to 1 by HW when a DEC_ERR response
is returend. 1=Error Valid. Software can write this bit to 0 to clear the logged errors (Clears valid,overflow,error-address bits).
- APS_AST_ERROR_ADDR_LO_0
- Offset: 0x8
- Read/Write: RO
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: AST_GBL_SEC_CONTROL_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:0 0x0 ErrAddrLo:
- RO: Lower 32 bits of the request that caused a decode error. This field is
not updated if AST_ERROR_STATUS[Valid]=1
- APS_AST_ERROR_ADDR_HI_0
- Offset: 0xc
- Read/Write: RO
- Parity Protection: N
- SCR Protection: AST_GBL_SEC_CONTROL_0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:0 0x0 ErrAddrHi:
- RO: Error Address high: When a DEC_ERR response is returned and
- AST_ERROR_STATUS[Valid]=0 the upper 32 bits of the error address are
latched into this register. This field is not updated if AST_ERROR_STATUS[Valid]=1.
- APS_AST_STREAMID_CTL_0
This is an array of 16 identical register entries; the register fields below apply to each entry.
- Offset: 0x20,..,0x5c
Read/Write: See table below.
- Parity Protection: N

- AON Registers

- Reset: 0x00000000 (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,0000,0000,xxxx,xxx0)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
15:8 0x0
- StreamID: RW: This specifies the StreamID output when the VMIndx field
is programmed to N in a region control register. N is the STREAMID_CTL register number 0x0
- RW: VM Index Enable: When this bit is set StreamID[N] can be selected
by the VMIndx N. 1=VM Index enabled, 0=VM Index disabled. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE
- APS_AST_WRITE_BLOCK_STATUS_0
- Offset: 0x60
- Read/Write: RO
- Parity Protection: N

- Reset: 0xXXXXXXXX (0bxxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx,xxxx)

- Description
31:0 X
- WRITE_BLOCK_STATUS
RW:
- WriteBlockStatus [0] = outstanding_w_flushed,
- WriteBlockStatus [1] = outstanding_r_flushed,
- WriteBlockStatus [5:2] = block_cur_st,
- WriteBlockStatus [9:6] = beat_count,
- WriteBlockStatus [10] = hold_w,
- WriteBlockStatus [11] = outstanding_w_max,
- WriteBlockStatus [12] = outstanding_r_max,
- WriteBlockStatus [31:13] = 0

- ReadBlockStatus[9:6] = beat_count,
- ReadBlockStatus[10] = hold_w,
